hero.headline: Immigration proceedings. Conducted to conclusion.
hero.subheadline: We represent individual and corporate clients in proceedings for visas, residence permits, work authorization and Polish citizenship. One dedicated team per matter. One point of accountability. One outcome.
hero.microcopy: Initial conversation — complimentary, up to 30 minutes, no commitment.

about.headline: A pioneering approach to immigration law.
paragraphs: Polskie Centrum Wizowe is an elite boutique advisory firm specializing in immigration and administrative law. We provide strategic counsel to individuals, families and international corporate groups whose status demands impeccable legal and operational command.

We operate within a multi-disciplinary model. Each engagement is structured around dedicated attorneys-at-law (adwokaci), legal advisors (radcowie prawni) and experts in international markets. The integrity of the entire process — from initial risk analysis to final resolution — is personally secured by a designated Managing Partner.

Our practice focuses on matters of the highest degree of complexity: multi-jurisdictional, combining immigration procedures with international corporate law, and requiring representation before key state authorities and inspection services.
Our foundation is absolute discretion, clarity of intent and full procedural accountability. We accept only a limited number of matters per year, guaranteeing our Clients absolute priority.
about.highlight_sublabel: The best measure of our reliability

services.headline: Full scope of immigration proceedings
services.subheadline: From visas and residence permits to citizenship and appeals — we handle every stage of the proceedings.

items.title: Employment visas
items.description: End-to-end handling of visa procedures for foreign nationals taking up employment in Poland. We conduct national D-type visa proceedings together with work authorization and consular documentation. Our team coordinates between employer and employee, eliminating procedural risk at every stage — from eligibility audit to issuance of the decision.

items.title: Business visas
items.description: Advisory services for entrepreneurs, executives and individuals seconded to Poland. We handle Schengen C-type and national D-type visas for business purposes, manage the formal aspects of secondment, and coordinate procedures with HR departments of parent entities. We work both with Polish inviting parties and foreign corporate groups.

items.title: Student visas
items.description: Support for individuals pursuing studies at Polish universities. We conduct visa proceedings and prepare clients for temporary residence permits for the purpose of education. We coordinate with universities on required documentation and with consulates on procedural matters, taking on the full administrative workload.

items.title: Temporary residence permit
items.description: Administrative proceedings before the voivode (regional governor) with jurisdiction over the foreign national's place of residence. We provide full legal analysis of permit grounds, draft the application, compile supporting documentation and represent the client throughout the proceedings. We handle matters under all statutory grounds: employment, business activity, studies, residence with a family member of a Polish national, and other grounds prescribed by law.

items.title: Permanent residence permit
items.description: Available to individuals meeting statutory criteria — including spouses of Polish citizens, holders of the Pole's Card (Karta Polaka) and persons of Polish origin. We conduct detailed eligibility analysis, compile supporting documentation, and represent the client before the voivode. Practical outcome of a granted permit: indefinite right of residence and eligibility to apply for Polish citizenship.

items.title: EU long-term resident permit
items.description: A status established under Council Directive 2003/109/EC, granting long-term residence rights across EU member states. We handle the full procedure: verification of lawful residence period, income and Polish language proficiency, application drafting and representation before the voivode. The status enables transfer of one's centre of life to another EU member state under a simplified regime.

items.title: Polish citizenship
items.description: Two distinct procedures: grant of citizenship by the President of the Republic of Poland, and recognition as a Polish citizen by the voivode. We assess the optimal route for each situation, draft the substantive reasoning of the application, and compile evidence of integration with the Republic. In complex cases, we also conduct proceedings confirming existing Polish citizenship — relevant in historical and lineage-based situations.

items.title: Representation before the National Labour Inspectorate and Border Guard
items.description: Representation of employers and foreign nationals in inspection proceedings conducted by the National Labour Inspectorate (Państwowa Inspekcja Pracy / PIP) and the Border Guard (Straż Graniczna). Scope includes attendance during inspections, drafting of written explanations and objections, representation in administrative penalty and appeal proceedings. We also conduct internal compliance audits regarding foreign national employment — a regulatory risk-prevention measure for employers of larger scale.

items.title: Work authorization
items.description: Handling of all work permit types: A, B, C, D and E; seasonal permits type S; statements of intent to entrust work (oświadczenia o powierzeniu); and applications under the EU Blue Card framework. We conduct procedures in full coordination with the employer, managing obligations toward the voivode, the labour office (urząd pracy) and inspection authorities. Every matter is closed with a wrap-up report containing recommendations for subsequent hires.

private_client.headline: Private Client
private_client.description: A dedicated legal ecosystem for clients of substantial means. The Private Client line was established for Clients whose legal, business and personal status requires holistic coordination beyond standard procedural frameworks. We serve entrepreneurs, investors, C-suite executives and family offices managing complex wealth across multiple jurisdictions. Every relationship is overseen by an individual Relationship Counsel, who coordinates the work of a consortium of experts — from immigration and tax law to corporate structuring.
scope: Family and personal relocation: full logistical coordination, including continuity of education and private residential arrangements.
Structuring of business presence: analysis and implementation of optimal legal forms for foreign capital in Poland.
International consolidation: seamless cooperation with the Client's foreign advisors — banking, legal and tax.
Prevention and legal protection: ongoing assistance in administrative, inspection and crisis matters.
Status architecture: long-term planning of legal positioning — from residence to citizenship.
private_client.operating_mode: Operating mode: absolute confidentiality, a single point of contact, availability 24/7/365. Work is conducted under project methodology, ensuring the predictability of milestones.
private_client.engagement_note: Engagement within the Private Client line is of an elite character. The terms of engagement and fee structure are defined individually following an initial audit of intent.

steps.title: Initial consultation
steps.description: Analysis of the client's legal situation, assessment of available procedural routes and determination of the optimal strategy. Online or in-person consultation.

steps.title: Analysis and case strategy
steps.description: Preparation of a detailed procedural plan, list of required documents and timeline. Documentation verification and gap-filling.

steps.title: Representation before authorities
steps.description: Filing the application, active representation before the administrative body, ongoing case monitoring and response to official requests and correspondence.

steps.title: Decision and post-proceeding support
steps.description: Receipt of decision, analysis and next steps — including appeal if warranted. Support through subsequent stages (extension, permanent residence, citizenship).

items.quote: "They moved seven people from three jurisdictions through relocation in a single quarter. What stayed with me was that I never had to ask 'what's next.' Every week, an updated status. For an HR function running five other projects in parallel, that has real value."
items.author: T. W.

items.quote: "A citizenship recognition matter that I had previously attempted twice on my own. Counsel identified three issues that no one had flagged before. Positive decision in the fourteenth month. Without their precision, I would probably still be trying."
items.author: Karol M.

items.quote: "An engagement requiring coordination with our tax practice in Zurich, our immigration counsel in London and the team in Warsaw. I recommend without reservation."
items.author: Managing Partner

items.quote: "The first matter where no one promised me anything in the first call. They told me exactly what the risks were, what could be done and how long it would take. That was the moment I knew I had come to the right place."
items.author: Yulia K.

items.quote: "After twenty years of operating in Italy, I decided to move my centre of activity to Poland. The procedure was conducted in full, with communication exclusively in Italian through a dedicated team member. What mattered most: no one told me 'this is how it's done in Poland.' They explained how we would do it in my particular matter."
items.author: A. B.

items.quote: "EU Blue Card in seven weeks. My employer said it was the fastest he had seen."
items.author: R. P.

items.quote: "After the sale of my business in Asia, I was looking for a neutral, stable European jurisdiction. The team helped me understand why Poland — rather than Portugal or Malta — would be a sensible choice for the coming decade. It was a consultation I will remember."
items.author: S. L.

items.quote: "A sensitive matter requiring full discretion. Every condition I requested was observed. I have nothing to add, other than that I have recommended onward."
items.author: Private client

items.quote: "We relocated three board members and their families from Frankfurt to Warsaw — nine people, including school-aged children. The team coordinated not only the visa procedures, but also advised on international schools and residential properties. Without their work, the project would have stretched out by six months."
items.author: H. K.

items.quote: "We collaborate regularly on matters requiring coordination with the U.S. immigration system. The quality of work is comparable to firms we work with in London and Vienna."
items.author: Partner

items.quote: "A difficult matter — very difficult. I received what I had been waiting twenty-seven years for."
items.author: Y. D.

items.quote: "Poland is strategic for our fund. The team guided us through immigration processes and integrated them with our holding structure in Luxembourg. The added value was not obvious — and it was material."
items.author: Founder

items.quote: "Forty production workers in a single quarter. Without a single negative decision."
items.author: HR Director

items.quote: "Polish immigration procedures are not intuitive to someone accustomed to the Portuguese-language system. They explained everything patiently, twice. Positive decision."
items.author: M. S.

items.quote: "Three years after the first consultation, the same team is handling my permanent residence proceedings. Continuity of representation is something that cannot be bought after the fact."
items.author: J. K.

footer.tagline: Boutique immigration and administrative law firm.

legal.copyright: © 2025 Polskie Centrum Wizowe. All rights reserved.
legal.disclaimer: Polskie Centrum Wizowe is a private advisory firm. We are not a public administration body of the Republic of Poland.
legal.disclaimer_full: Polskie Centrum Wizowe is a private commercial entity providing advisory services on commercial terms. We are not a public administration body of the Republic of Poland, a unit of the public finance sector, nor an entity organizationally or capital-affiliated with the State Treasury. Our services constitute legal and administrative advisory; proceedings for visas, residence permits, work authorization and Polish citizenship are conducted by the competent administrative authorities — consulates, voivodes, the Office for Foreigners (Urząd do Spraw Cudzoziemców), the President of the Republic of Poland, and other authorities empowered by statute.

cookies.title: We use cookies
cookies.description: We use cookies to improve your experience and analyse site traffic. You may accept all or choose necessary only. Learn more in our

sections.title: § 1. General Provisions
paragraphs: These Terms of Service (hereinafter: the Terms) define the rules for the provision of advisory services by Polskie Centrum Wizowe (hereinafter: the Centre) to clients (hereinafter: the Client).

Polskie Centrum Wizowe is a private commercial entity. It is not a public administration body of the Republic of Poland, a unit of the public finance sector, nor an entity organizationally or capital-affiliated with the State Treasury.
The Centre provides legal and administrative advisory services related to immigration law, visa procedures, residence permits, work authorisation, Polish citizenship, and related matters.
These Terms are available at the Centre's registered office and on its website. Before entering into a service agreement, the Client is obliged to read the Terms.

sections.title: § 2. Scope of Services
paragraphs: The Centre provides advisory services including: analysis of the Client's situation and eligibility for specific procedures, preparation and review of applications and supporting documents, representation before administrative authorities (where applicable), ongoing information on the status of proceedings, and other services specified in individual agreements.
The Centre does not guarantee a specific outcome of administrative proceedings, as final decisions are made exclusively by competent state authorities.
The scope of services in each case is defined in a written service agreement concluded with the Client.

sections.title: § 3. Contract Formation
paragraphs: A service agreement is concluded upon signing a written contract by both parties or upon confirmation of the order in a form accepted by the Centre.
Prior to concluding the agreement, the Centre provides the Client with full information on the scope of services, expected timeline, fees, and any risks associated with the proceedings.

The Centre reserves the right to refuse to conclude an agreement in justified cases, in particular where the Client's declared situation is inconsistent with applicable law or the Centre's ethical standards.

sections.title: § 4. Client Rights
paragraphs: The Client has the right to be fully and reliably informed about the status of their case at every stage of service provision.
The Client has the right to access all documents prepared by the Centre in connection with the provision of services.
The Client has the right to withdraw from the agreement at any time, subject to the terms set out in § 11 of these Terms.
The Client has the right to lodge a complaint in accordance with the procedure set out in § 11 of these Terms.

sections.title: § 5. Client Obligations
paragraphs: The Client is obliged to provide truthful and complete information and documents necessary for proper performance of the service.
The Client is obliged to promptly inform the Centre of any changes in their situation that may affect the course of proceedings.
The Client is obliged to pay the agreed remuneration on the dates and in the amounts specified in the service agreement.
The Client is obliged to cooperate with the Centre and to follow its guidance in matters necessary for effective conduct of the proceedings.

sections.title: § 6. Centre Obligations
paragraphs: The Centre undertakes to provide services with due diligence, in accordance with applicable law and current professional standards.
The Centre undertakes to keep confidential all information and documents obtained from the Client in connection with service provision.
The Centre undertakes to inform the Client without undue delay of any obstacles or circumstances that may affect the course or outcome of the proceedings.
The Centre undertakes to act exclusively in the Client's interest throughout the duration of the service agreement.

sections.title: § 7. Fees and Payment Terms
paragraphs: The remuneration for services is specified in the individual service agreement concluded with the Client.
The Centre applies transparent fee policies. All fees are disclosed to the Client prior to concluding the agreement.
Remuneration is generally payable in instalments: an advance payment upon conclusion of the agreement, and subsequent payments at stages agreed by the parties.
Fees for services do not include official charges, consular fees, or other public charges imposed by administrative authorities, unless expressly stated in the agreement.
In the event of withdrawal from the agreement by the Client, the rules for refunding or retaining amounts paid are set out in § 11 of these Terms.

sections.title: § 8. Liability
paragraphs: The Centre is liable to the Client for non-performance or improper performance of the service agreement to the extent provided for in applicable law.
The Centre is not liable for decisions taken by administrative authorities, which are independent in their actions and not subject to the Centre's influence.
The Centre is not liable for consequences resulting from the Client providing false, incomplete, or misleading information or documents.
The Centre's liability is limited to the amount of the remuneration actually received for the service in question, unless mandatory provisions of law provide otherwise.

sections.title: § 9. Confidentiality
paragraphs: The Centre undertakes to maintain the confidentiality of all information obtained from the Client in connection with the provision of services.
The confidentiality obligation shall remain in force after the termination of the service agreement, without any time limitation.

The confidentiality obligation does not apply to information that: is publicly available through lawful channels, was obtained from a third party without a confidentiality obligation, or must be disclosed under mandatory provisions of law.

sections.title: § 10. Personal Data Protection
paragraphs: The Centre processes the Client's personal data as the data controller within the meaning of Regulation (EU) 2016/679 of the European Parliament and of the Council (GDPR).

Detailed information on the processing of personal data, including purposes, legal bases, retention periods, and data subjects' rights, is set out in the Privacy Policy available on the Centre's website.
By concluding a service agreement, the Client confirms they have read the Privacy Policy.

sections.title: § 11. Complaints and Withdrawal
paragraphs: The Client has the right to lodge a complaint regarding the quality of services provided. Complaints should be submitted in writing to the Centre's registered office address or by electronic means.
The Centre undertakes to consider the complaint within 14 working days of its receipt and to inform the Client of the outcome.
The Client may withdraw from the service agreement at any time. In the event of withdrawal before the commencement of service provision, the advance payment will be refunded in full.

In the event of withdrawal after services have commenced, the Centre is entitled to remuneration for services actually performed up to the date of withdrawal. Any remaining amount shall be refunded within 14 days.

sections.title: § 12. Final Provisions
paragraphs: These Terms shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the law of the Republic of Poland.

Any disputes arising out of or in connection with the provision of services shall be resolved by the competent common court having jurisdiction over the Centre's registered office, unless the parties agree otherwise.
The Centre reserves the right to amend these Terms. Any amendments shall be communicated to Clients in advance, in writing or by electronic means, with at least 14 days' notice.
Matters not regulated by these Terms shall be governed by the provisions of the Polish Civil Code and other applicable legislation.

polityka_prywatnosci.title: Privacy Policy
polityka_prywatnosci.subtitle: Personal Data Protection in Accordance with GDPR

sections.title: 1. Data Controller
paragraphs: The controller of your personal data is Polskie Centrum Wizowe, a private commercial advisory company operating in the Republic of Poland.
You may contact us regarding matters related to the processing of personal data by post at our registered office address or by electronic mail at the address indicated on the website.

sections.title: 2. Purposes and Legal Bases of Processing
paragraphs: We process your personal data for the following purposes: (a) performance of the service agreement concluded with you — legal basis: Article 6(1)(b) GDPR; (b) fulfilling our legal obligations — legal basis: Article 6(1)(c) GDPR; (c) our legitimate interests, in particular the investigation and defence of claims — legal basis: Article 6(1)(f) GDPR; (d) sending marketing communications — legal basis: your consent under Article 6(1)(a) GDPR.
Providing data is voluntary but necessary for the conclusion and performance of the service agreement. Failure to provide data may prevent us from providing services.

sections.title: 3. Categories of Data Processed
paragraphs: We process the following categories of personal data: identification data (name, surname, date of birth, PESEL number or document number), contact data (address, telephone number, e-mail address), data contained in documents submitted in connection with the proceedings (including data from passports and other travel documents), and data concerning the immigration situation.
We do not process special categories of personal data (sensitive data) unless their processing is strictly necessary to perform the service and you have given your explicit consent.

sections.title: 4. Data Retention Periods
paragraphs: We retain your personal data for the period necessary to perform the service agreement and — after its termination — for the period required by law (including tax and accounting obligations) or until the expiry of the limitation periods for claims.
Data processed on the basis of consent is retained until the consent is withdrawn.

sections.title: 5. Recipients of Personal Data
paragraphs: Your data may be disclosed to: competent administrative authorities (consulates, voivodes, the Office for Foreigners, and other bodies) in connection with the conduct of proceedings on your behalf; IT service providers and other entities processing data on our instructions on the basis of data processing agreements; legal advisors and auditors, where required by law.
We do not sell or share your personal data with third parties for marketing purposes.

sections.title: 6. Data Subject Rights
paragraphs: You have the following rights with respect to the processing of your personal data: the right of access to your data, the right to rectification, the right to erasure, the right to restriction of processing, the right to data portability, and the right to object to processing.
If processing is based on your consent, you may withdraw that consent at any time without affecting the lawfulness of processing prior to the withdrawal.
You have the right to lodge a complaint with the President of the Office for Personal Data Protection (UODO) if you consider that the processing of your data infringes GDPR.

sections.title: 7. International Data Transfers
paragraphs: In connection with the conduct of proceedings before consulates or other authorities located outside the European Economic Area, your data may be transferred to third countries.

Such transfers are carried out only to the extent necessary to perform the service and are subject to appropriate safeguards, including standard contractual clauses or other mechanisms provided for in GDPR.

sections.title: 8. Cookies
paragraphs: Our website uses cookies — small text files stored on your device that allow us to provide a better user experience.

We use the following types of cookies: necessary cookies (essential for the website to function, always active) and analytical cookies (anonymous traffic and behaviour data collected via Google Analytics 4).

Analytical cookies are activated only after you give your consent through the banner displayed on your first visit. Until consent is granted, Google Analytics operates in anonymous mode with limited data collection (Consent Mode v2). You may withdraw or change your consent at any time by clearing the pcw_cookie_consent key from your browser's local storage or through your browser's cookie settings.
You may manage cookies through your browser settings. Disabling some cookies may affect the functionality of the website.
For more information about Google Analytics and the opt-out option, visit tools.google.com/dlpage/gaoptout.

sections.title: 9. Data Security
paragraphs: We apply technical and organisational measures to protect your personal data against unauthorised access, loss, or disclosure, including data encryption, access controls, and regular security reviews.
Our employees who process personal data are bound by confidentiality obligations and have received appropriate training in data protection.

sections.title: 10. Changes to the Policy
paragraphs: We reserve the right to update this Privacy Policy. Any changes will be published on our website with the date of the amendment indicated.
If significant changes are made that may affect your rights, we will additionally inform you by e-mail or by prominent notice on the website.

sections.title: 11. Contact
paragraphs: For all matters relating to the processing of your personal data, please contact us at the address indicated on the website or by electronic mail.
We will respond to your enquiry within 30 days of receipt, or — in complex cases — within such longer period as permitted by applicable law, having first informed you of the reason for the delay.
